# Visual Redesign Spec — DS Portal Home Page

**Document:** Visual/structural redesign proposal for `docs/index.html` (generated by `docs/build_portal.py` → `render_shell()` + `body_home()`)
**Based on:** Live review of cartrack-design-portal.vercel.app/index.html (2026-07-21), the `brand/` folder (Logo, Colour, Typography, Icons, Graphic Design), `tokens/tokens.json`, and the studio-design guidance in the attached `frontend-design` skill.
**Status:** Proposal — not yet built. Content is unchanged throughout; every change below is visual, structural, or layout.
**Scope:** Home page only (`index.html`). Extending this language to the rest of the portal is a follow-on decision, not part of this spec — see §7.

---

## 1. Purpose

The home page is the first thing a designer, developer, or AI agent sees before they've decided whether this system is worth adopting. Right now it reads as a competent, generic docs-site template — nothing on the page would tell you it belongs to Cartrack if the wordmark were removed. This spec proposes a visual identity for the home page that's actually derived from Cartrack's own brand (`brand/`) and its own product (fleet tracking), while leaving the sidebar navigation, the written content, and the underlying IA exactly as they are.

## 2. Current state — what's actually there

Read directly from `docs/index.html` / `build_portal.py`, not a screenshot:

- **Hero:** dark navy gradient block (`linear-gradient(150deg,#161d2b,#243147)`), headline, one paragraph, and four pill-shaped stat badges (`27 documented components`, `1 tokens source of truth`, `0 setup steps per session`, `MD2 · MDC 14 · React`). This is close to a template pattern the design-skill brief specifically calls out as overused: *"a big number with a small label, supporting stats, and a gradient accent."*
- **No brand mark anywhere.** The only branding is the text string "Cartrack AI Design System" in the sidebar. The logomark (arrow) isn't used once, despite `brand/Logo.doc.json` and `brand/GraphicDesign.doc.json` both documenting approved ways to do exactly that (small-format lockup, watermark).
- **Typography is 100% system-UI.** `--sans` is `-apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif` for every single piece of text — hero H1, body copy, nav labels, everything. There is no display face carrying any personality, even though `brand/Typography.doc.json` documents a real, deliberate pairing (Montserrat display + Roboto body) for exactly this kind of brand-facing surface.
- **Three "path" cards (Designers / Developers / AI agents)** are visually identical — same white card, same border, only the text differs. The one piece of information that's actually useful at a glance (which path is which) isn't encoded visually at all.
- **Getting Started** uses generic numbered circle badges (`1`, `2`) — not wrong (the two steps really are sequential: download, then connect), but styled with no relationship to the brand.
- **Colour drift from the design system it documents:** `--accent:#f4713a` in the portal's own CSS does not match `tokens.json` → `primitive.color.brand.orange.500` = `#F47735`. A design-system documentation site should not be off-token for its own accent colour. (`--accent-d:#bb4800` is correct — it matches `primitive.color.brand.orange.700` exactly.)
- **No colour in the page traces back to either `tokens.json` or `brand/`.** `--ink:#141a24` (a navy-black), `--bg:#faf9f7` (a warm cream) and `--line:#e7e3dd` are all invented one-off values with no source — not brand black, not product charcoal, not the product's border token (`semantic.color.border.default` = `rgba(0,0,0,.12)`).
- **Radius values are inconsistent:** at least 8 distinct `border-radius` values in use (`3px, 4px, 7px, 8px, 10px, 12px, 14px`, plus `50%`/`999px` for circles/pills) with no visible system behind which value goes where.

None of this is broken — it's just undifferentiated. It would pass as the docs site for almost any B2B tool.

## 3. Design plan

Following the brainstorm → critique → build sequence: the plan below, then a critique of it against generic defaults, before any section-by-section detail.

### 3.1 Color — 5 named values, 4 of them already real

| Name | Hex | Source | Use |
|---|---|---|---|
| `brand-orange` | `#F47735` | `tokens.json` → `primitive.color.brand.orange.500` (fixes the current drift) | Accent line work, icons, the "Designers" path accent |
| `brand-orange-dark` | `#BB4800` | `tokens.json` → `primitive.color.brand.orange.700` | Buttons/links on light surfaces (AA-safe, already correct today) |
| `ink` | `#0C0C0C` | `tokens.json` → `primitive.color.brand.charcoal.900` | Replaces the invented navy — hero background, headline text. Reads as brand black, not a one-off "docs site navy." |
| `paper` | `#FFFFFF` / `primitive.color.hue.gray.10` | `tokens.json` | Replaces the invented cream `#faf9f7` — plain white body background, faint grey (`hue.gray.10`) for the sidebar/app chrome, matching how the product itself distinguishes `surface.default` from `surface.appBackground`. |
| `route-blue` | `#5390BC` *(placeholder — see §6)* | `tokens.json` → `primitive.color.hue.blue.base`, standing in for `brand/Colour.doc.json`'s unconfirmed brand blue | One accent only: the "AI agents" path card. Not used for text at small sizes until contrast is confirmed against the real brand hex. |

This isn't a bigger palette than before — it's the same small set of roles, corrected to values that actually exist in either `tokens.json` or the brand file, plus exactly one new colour (blue) used in exactly one place, matching `brand/Colour.doc.json`'s own "colour ratio" instruction: orange (and here, blue) is an accent, never a flood fill.

### 3.2 Type — two roles, both already documented in `brand/`

- **Display (Montserrat, 700/800, tight tracking):** hero H1, section H2s, the three nav category eyebrows (Guide / Library / Resources), the four stat values. Used with restraint — never body copy, per `brand/Typography.doc.json`'s own rule.
- **Body (existing system-sans stack, unchanged):** paragraphs, nav links, card copy. This already resolves to Roboto/Helvetica/Arial on most machines, which is also the product's real `semantic.typography.fontFamily` — correct as-is, no change needed here.
- **Mono (existing `ui-monospace` stack, unchanged):** code snippets, prompts. Already distinct and working.

Net new dependency: one display webfont (Montserrat, Google Fonts, Open Font License — see §6 for the loading question).

### 3.3 Layout — keep the skeleton, redesign the identity layer

The sidebar + main two-column layout, the filter box, the collapsible nav tree — none of that changes. It's a solved pattern for a reference site and reinventing it would cost usability for no brand benefit. What changes is the *identity layer* on top: hero, stat presentation, path cards, step styling.

```
BEFORE (hero)                          AFTER (hero)
┌──────────────────────────────┐       ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│  gradient navy block          │       │  ink-black block              │
│  H1 (system sans)             │       │  H1 (Montserrat)   [arrow     │
│  paragraph                    │       │  paragraph          watermark,│
│  [27] [1] [0] [MD2] ← 4 pills │       │  low-opacity,       │
│                                │       │  bottom-right]      │
└──────────────────────────────┘       │  ┈┈┈●───────────────●┈┈┈      │
                                        │  Download          Ship UI   │
                                        │  27 comp · 1 token · 0 setup │
                                        └──────────────────────────────┘
```

```
BEFORE (3 path cards)                   AFTER (3 path cards)
┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐        ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐
│ same   │ │ same   │ │ same   │        │orange  │ │ink     │ │blue    │
│ border │ │ border │ │ border │        │left rule│ │left rule│ │left rule│
│Designer│ │Devs    │ │AI agent│        │Designer│ │Devs    │ │AI agent│
└────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘        └────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘
```

### 3.4 Signature element — the route line

Cartrack's product is fleet tracking: routes, waypoints, live position. The home page's own content is already a route — download, then connect, then ship — so the getting-started flow is reframed literally as a route rather than generic numbered circles: a thin line running through the hero (ending in the two "Getting started" steps below it) with waypoint markers built from the arrow logomark instead of plain numbered dots. "Download" and "Ship UI" sit at the two ends. This is the one deliberately bold move on the page; everything else (cards, type, colour) stays quiet around it, per the "spend your boldness in one place" principle.

The two Getting Started step cards pick up the same line visually (a short connecting stroke between step 1 and step 2), so the motif appears exactly twice — hero and getting-started — and nowhere else on the page. It does not become a repeating decorative pattern.

### 3.5 Self-critique against generic defaults

Checked against the three AI-default clusters (cream+serif+terracotta / near-black+neon / broadsheet hairlines): this plan lands closest to "dark block + accent," which is the second default cluster — the risk is real. What keeps it from being that template: the accent is Cartrack's actual documented brand orange (not an invented vermilion/acid tone), the dark surface is brand black rather than a designed-for-drama near-black, and the one signature element (route line + logomark waypoints) is derived from the literal subject matter, not a generic geometric accent shape. If built and it still reads as "generic dark-hero SaaS site" once the route line is in place, the fallback is to drop the dark hero background entirely and run the route line on white instead — flagged in §6 as a build-time judgment call, not decided here.

Also deliberately **not** doing: no numbered treatment on the three path cards (they're parallel, not sequential — numbering them would encode false order); no new animation beyond one entrance draw of the route line; no fourth accent colour; no change to card corner-radius scale beyond collapsing existing values (see §5.6) — not introducing a new shape language.

## 4. Section-by-section changes

### 4.1 Hero
- Background: navy gradient → `ink` (`#0C0C0C`), flat or a very subtle darken-only gradient (no colour shift).
- Headline + eyebrow text: system sans → Montserrat 700.
- Add: arrow logomark, large scale, ~8–12% opacity, bottom-right corner, per the watermark treatment in `brand/GraphicDesign.doc.json` → Logomark (not the identification lockup rules in `Logo.doc.json` — different opacity/scale rules apply here).
- Add: the route line + two waypoint markers ("Download" / "Ship UI"), positioned between the paragraph and the stat strip.
- Stat badges: drop the glass-pill chrome (`.badge` styling); keep the same four facts as a plain inline strip, values in Montserrat, labels in body sans, no border/background per badge.

### 4.2 Getting Started steps
- Keep both step cards and their copy verbatim.
- Add a short vertical connector between the two `.stepc` cards, visually continuing the hero's route line.
- Replace the plain numbered circle with the same waypoint-marker treatment used in the hero (small arrow mark, not a bare digit) — one motif, two sizes, not two different devices.

### 4.3 Three path cards (Designers / Developers / AI agents)
- Keep all copy and the prompt/example blocks verbatim.
- Add a left-edge colour rule per card: orange (Designers), ink/charcoal (Developers), route-blue (AI agents) — a direct application of the "one accent colour per grouping" logic in `brand/Colour.doc.json` → Colour combinations, and it makes the three-way choice scannable without adding any new copy.
- No numbering — these are parallel entry points, not a sequence.

### 4.4 Warning callout
- No visual change proposed — the existing `.warn` block (amber background, already close to the product's warning token family) is fine as-is and out of scope.

### 4.5 Global token cleanup (affects the whole page, not just one section)
- Fix `--accent` from `#f4713a` to the real `#F47735`.
- Replace `--ink`, `--bg`, `--line` with the sourced values in §3.1.
- Collapse the 8 ad hoc `border-radius` values to 3: `10px` (standard cards/inputs), `14px` (hero-level / feature cards), `999px` (pills, already used correctly for badges/chips). Anything currently at `3px/4px/6px/7px/8px/9px/12px/16px` moves to the nearest of the three.

## 5. What this spec deliberately does not touch

- Sidebar navigation structure, filter box, collapsible tree behaviour — unchanged.
- Page content/copy — unchanged, verbatim, as requested.
- Any page other than `index.html` — foundations/tokens/accessibility/components pages are out of scope until this direction is approved (see §7).
- The product design system (`tokens/tokens.json`, `components/`) — this spec only touches the documentation portal's own presentation layer, never the tokens/components it documents.

## 6. Open questions before build

1. **Brand blue hex is still unconfirmed.** `brand/Colour.doc.json` flags the real brand blue as `TBD — verify against Figma`. §3.1 uses the product's existing muted `hue.blue.base` (`#5390BC`) as a stand-in. Before building, either confirm the real brand blue from Figma (needs the file open in Figma desktop with a layer selected — see the tooling note in `brand/BRAND.md`) or accept the stand-in.
2. **Font loading:** Montserrat via Google Fonts CDN (one `<link>`, matches how the brand file itself likely sources it) vs. self-hosting a subset. The portal currently has zero external requests; this is a real new dependency worth a conscious yes/no, not an assumed one.
3. **Dark vs. light hero fallback** if the built result still reads as a generic "dark hero" template once in the browser — see the self-critique in §3.5. Recommend actually building it and taking a screenshot before deciding, rather than pre-deciding from a spec.
4. **Rollout scope** — see §7.

## 7. Follow-on (not part of this spec)

If this direction is approved, the same token corrections (§3.1, §4.5) should extend to every other portal page for consistency — they currently share the same `render_shell()` CSS block, so the color/radius fixes are effectively global already once made once. The Montserrat display face, route-line motif, and path-card colour-coding are home-page-specific signature choices and should **not** be copy-pasted onto every component/pattern page — those pages are reference material, not a landing experience, and should stay quiet.

## 8. Implementation notes (for whoever builds this)

- `docs/index.html` is generated, not hand-authored — never edit it directly. Changes go in `docs/build_portal.py`: shared CSS lives in `render_shell()` (~line 340), home-page markup in `body_home()` (~line 575).
- Re-run the generator and diff every other output page after touching `render_shell()`, since its CSS is shared across all pages even though only the home page's *markup* is changing in this spec.
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` for the route-line entrance animation — fall back to the line rendered fully in place, no draw-in.
- Verify the new blue accent (once real hex is confirmed) against white/ink backgrounds for WCAG AA before using it for anything but a decorative rule/icon.
